learning_outcomes.description: Understand what social entrepreneurship is and the scope of the field
Clearly articulate personal values and how they relate to wider business, community and global issues and outcomes
Develop a social impact model for a specific social issue while working in teams
Plan, execute and evaluate a social business model in entrepreneurial teams whilst managing diverse viewpoints, needs and outcomes

assessments.assessment_title: Test the Problem + Levers of Change: Group Video and Report
assessments.due_date:
Map the System Worksheet 1 6/03/2026 5:00 pm

Map the System Worksheet 2 13/03/2026 5:00 pm

Map the System Worksheet 3 13/03/2026 5:00 pm

Map the System Worksheet 4 20/03/2026 5:00 pm

Map the System Worksheet 5 27/03/2026 5:00 pm

Map the System Worksheet 6 27/03/2026 5:00 pm

Group Video and Report 21/04/2026 5:00 pm

Worksheets due end of Seminar
assessments.task_description:
Students will form groups and are expected to complete the following tasks:

  • Students will complete 6 Map the Systems worksheets, to be submitted at the end of the seminar for the relevant week.
  • Students will develop systems maps and synthesise research surrounding a chosen societal issue, exposing levers of change that are suitable for a social enterprise/not-for-profit to address. Using this insight, students will develop a synthesised PCSM (problem-cause-strategy-measure) model that will inform solution development, identifying three levers of change that will be explored in the solution review. Students will submit this as a report (developed using tools of their choice; i.e. Kumu [systems maps], PowerPoint, Prezi, Canva).
  • Students will report on and defend findings in a 10-minute video to be submitted via Blackboard.

Detailed assessment guidelines will be available on Blackboard.

Can I use Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) for this assignment? 

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Worksheets must be completed in hard copy at the end of the seminar and uploaded to Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Test the Solution: Group Presentation and Report
assessments.due_date:
21/05/2026 5:00 pm

All materials must be uploaded by the due date. Presentations will occur across weeks 12 and 13.
assessments.task_description:
Students will be expected to explore three levers for change that can intervene in a chosen system, connecting to the problem framing developed by team in Assessment 1. Students will justify the selection of one lever for change that is well-suited to a social enterprise business model, demonstrating prospect of business sustainability in both financial and social dimensions, and explore unintended consequences of social intervention.

Students will report on and defend findings in a 10-minute presentation and Q&A in-class, and submit their analysis in the form of a report/slide appendices.

Detailed assessment guidelines will be available via Blackboard.

Please Note: The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.

Can I use Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) for this assignment? 

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Presentation materials and report must be uploaded to Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction to Social Entrepreneurship

An overview of the course, its content, and assessments.


Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: Introduction to Social Entrepreneurship An overview of the course, its content, and assessments.

learning_activities.topic:
Defining Scope for Social Entrepreneurship

This seminar focuses on project areas for social entrepreneurship, an approach to understanding systems, and forming successful teams.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Defining Scope for Social Entrepreneurship This seminar focuses on project areas for social entrepreneurship, an approach to understanding systems, and forming successful teams.

learning_activities.topic:
Problem Definition

This seminar focuses on defining an appropriate social problem to examine as a team.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Problem Definition This seminar focuses on defining an appropriate social problem to examine as a team.

learning_activities.topic:
Social Impact Model - Part 1

This seminar introduces a structured social impact model. We will focus on tools to identify and systematically understand causes and impacts of social problems.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Social Impact Model - Part 1 This seminar introduces a structured social impact model. We will focus on tools to identify and systematically understand causes and impacts of social problems.

learning_activities.topic:
Social Impact Model - Part 2

This seminar focuses on employing systems analysis tools to understanding social impact. We will explore the tools to examine the persistence of problems, the (in)effectiveness of existing solutions, and possible solutions.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Social Impact Model - Part 2 This seminar focuses on employing systems analysis tools to understanding social impact. We will explore the tools to examine the persistence of problems, the (in)effectiveness of existing solutions, and possible solutions.

learning_activities.topic:
Intervening in Systems - Assignment 1 Review

We will summarize our approach to date in examining social entrepreneurship as a systems change activity.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Intervening in Systems - Assignment 1 Review We will summarize our approach to date in examining social entrepreneurship as a systems change activity.

learning_activities.topic:
Developing a Social Business Model

We will introduce business model approaches for social enterprises.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Developing a Social Business Model We will introduce business model approaches for social enterprises.

learning_activities.topic:
Social Impact Measurement

This seminar focuses on measuring social impact and systematically examining how different interventions can be assessed. We will consider unintended consequences of social impact.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Social Impact Measurement This seminar focuses on measuring social impact and systematically examining how different interventions can be assessed. We will consider unintended consequences of social impact.

learning_activities.topic:
Social Business Finance

This seminar focuses on innovations in social business financing.

Learning outcomes: L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Social Business Finance This seminar focuses on innovations in social business financing.

learning_activities.topic:
Speculative Futures & Course Review

This seminar focuses on tools for perceiving and managing unintended consequences based on futures thinking.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Speculative Futures & Course Review This seminar focuses on tools for perceiving and managing unintended consequences based on futures thinking.
